summary: GrantDrop verifies its Groth16 eligibility proof on-chain in a deployed Soroban contract using the native BN254 pairing host functions (Stellar Protocol 25, CAP-0074). The accepted claim is gated on a real, signed testnet verify_claim invocation.

verify_claim_invocations.label: Live in-browser claim on the production site (https://grantdrop-stellar-zk.pages.dev, client testnet signer)

trust_boundary.on_chain: Soroban verify_claim runs the BN254 Groth16 pairing check and the secretSquare eligibility check; the accepted receipt requires this signed invocation to return true.
trust_boundary.browser: Generates the Groth16 proof (snarkjs) and fast-fails ineligible input for UX before any transaction.
trust_boundary.stellar: A signed manageData transaction anchors the public receipt marker.
